intro: Hakea squiresiae, also known as the Squire's hakea, is a species of shrub native to Western Australia. Traditionally used in Indigenous Australian medicine, Hakea squiresiae has been recognized for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing and the management of respiratory issues. The plant contains a range of bioactive constituents, including alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
